notes: All assertions pass for both configurations — the quantitative pass rate is tied at 100%.

The with-skill runs use fewer tokens on average (-3985), especially for explain-deploy-flow (21970 vs 30796) where the skill provided architecture context directly instead of needing 24 tool calls to discover it.
The with-skill runs are slower on wall-clock time on average (+9.6s), likely due to reading the skill file adding overhead.

The qualitative differences are more interesting: the with-skill add-secret run actually modified infra-doc files (documentation protocol), while the baseline only described what to do. The with-skill check-services run was faster but may have compared against the server's copy of deploy-test rather than the git branch, missing a potential mismatch the baseline caught.
The explain-deploy-flow outputs are comparable in quality, but the with-skill version is significantly more detailed (136 lines vs 90 lines) and includes design decisions and timing estimates.
